title: Gate Handoff Metadata Schema (v0.2.0)
description: Frozen specification for gate output metadata including risk scoring, architecture quality proxies, violations, warnings, escalation reasons, and audit event references. Extends M902-01 v0.1.0 with risk-based escalation semantics. Backward compatible via version field; consumers must handle version mismatches gracefully.

_comment.description: Schema documentation and backward compatibility notes. v0.2.0 extends M902-01 v0.1.0. Consumers must ignore unknown fields for forward compatibility.
_comment.default: Handoff metadata schema v0.2.0 (M902-04). Extends M902-01 v0.1.0 with risk scoring and escalation. Consumers must handle version mismatches by ignoring unknown fields.

version.description: Schema version (semantic versioning). v0.1.0 = M902-01 base; v0.2.0 = M902-04 with escalation.

status.description: Overall gate result status. PASS = no violations; WARN = moderate violations; FAIL = high violations; ESCALATE = human review required.

risk_score.description: Numeric risk score (0-100) or UNKNOWN string. Weighted average of violation severities. Thresholds: <=50=PASS, >50-75=WARN, >75-90=FAIL, >90=ESCALATE.

architecture_score.description: Numeric architecture quality score (0-100) or UNKNOWN. Penalty-based: 100 - (AR violations * 10). Lower scores indicate worse architecture. Thresholds: >70=PASS, 60-70=WARN, 30-60=FAIL, <=30=ESCALATE.

test_confidence.description: Test confidence as percentage (0-100) or UNKNOWN. (tests_passed / tests_total) * 100 if gate has test data. Advisory only in MVP.

oneOf.description: No test data available (static analysis gates, etc.)

duplication_delta.description: Percentage change in code duplication from baseline (positive = more duplication) or null if unavailable. Formula: (current_tokens - baseline_tokens) / baseline_tokens * 100.

oneOf.description: Duplication delta unavailable (TODO: finalize baseline strategy in M903)

complexity_delta.description: Percentage change in code complexity from baseline (positive = more complex) or null if unavailable. Formula: (current_complexity - baseline_complexity) / baseline_complexity * 100.

oneOf.description: Complexity delta unavailable (TODO: finalize tool metrics in M903)

violations.description: Array of rule violations detected by gate. Each violation has rule_id, file, line, severity, message, remediation_hint, suppressible.

rule_id.description: Rule identifier (e.g., 'E501', 'AR-01', 'GV-06'). Prefix indicates tool: E/W=ruff, mypy-*=mypy, AR-*=architecture, GV-*=governance, etc.

file.description: File path relative to repo root (e.g., 'asset_generation/web/backend/routers/registry.py')

line.description: Line number (1-indexed) where violation occurs, or null if unknown/file-level

remediation_hint.description: Optional actionable hint for fixing the violation

oneOf.description: Remediation suggestion (e.g., 'Remove bare except clause; specify exception type')

suppressible.description: Whether this violation can be suppressed via comment (# noqa, # nosemgrep, etc.). Unsuppressible violations block merge.

warnings.description: Array of warnings (lower severity than violations). Same schema as violations.

escalation_reasons.description: Array of escalation triggers (detector results). Each escalation has detector, severity, details, confidence, recommendation.

severity.description: Escalation severity (different scale than rule severity; detector-specific)

confidence.description: Confidence level of escalation trigger (HIGH=deterministic, MEDIUM=heuristic, LOW=speculative)

recommendation.description: Recommended action (e.g., 'Design review required', 'Fix root cause or create ticket')

audit_events.description: Array of audit log entry references (paths + line numbers). Enables forensic tracing. References only; full events stored in audit logs.

violations.remediation_hint: Break line into multiple lines or use inline comment

violations.message: No newline at end of file
Domain module imports HTTP library: fastapi
violations.remediation_hint: Remove fastapi import; move HTTP logic to adapter/router layer

violations.message: Service layer constructs HTTP response directly
violations.remediation_hint: Move response construction to router layer; service should return data only

violations.message: Exception handler swallows error silently (except: pass)
violations.remediation_hint: Log exception or re-raise; silent swallowing is dangerous

warnings.message: Suppression without issue link: # noqa: E501

violations.message: Domain module imports HTTP library: requests
violations.remediation_hint: Remove requests import; use adapter pattern for network I/O

escalation_reasons.details: Governance file CLAUDE.md modified with architecture rule AR-02: Architecture rule violation in policy file
escalation_reasons.recommendation: Design review required before merge. Verify governance file changes are intentional.

escalation_reasons.details: New architecture violations detected: AR-01. This rule was not present in baseline.
escalation_reasons.recommendation: Fix new architecture violations before merge. These indicate structural boundary violations.

examples.description: Example 5: Multi-tool aggregated output (combined ruff + mypy + sempreg violations)

violations.message: Argument 1 to 'dict' has incompatible type 'list[str]'; expected 'SupportsKeysAndGetItem[str, Unknown]'
violations.remediation_hint: Fix type annotation or cast appropriately

violations.message: Use of getattr() outside allowed reflection zone
violations.remediation_hint: Move getattr() to router, serializer, or test zone; or use factory pattern
